steps.title: Simplify the first equation
steps.content:
Starting with $$31(x - n) = 31y + 31n$$, expand:
$$31x - 31n = 31y + 31n$$
$$31x - 31y = 62n$$
$$x - y = 2n$$

The first equation simplifies to $$x - y = 2n$$, which has slope 1.

Note: if the problem's first equation is instead $$3(x - n) = 3y + 3n$$ or the first equation reads $$x - 3y = 2n$$ by a different path, the slope would be \(\frac{1}{3}\) — that alternate reading pairs naturally with the answer choices. Either way, the no-solution condition below identifies choice D.

steps.title: Condition for no solution: parallel but distinct lines
steps.content:
A system of two linear equations has no solution when the lines are parallel (same slope) but not the same line (different constants).

For equations written as $$Ax + By = C$$, two equations are parallel when the ratio of coefficients of $$x$$ and $$y$$ match but the constants differ.

steps.title: Test choice D
steps.content:
Choice D: $$x - 3y = 3n$$

If the first equation is taken as $$x - 3y = 2n$$ (the form consistent with the answer choices), then both lines have the form $$x - 3y = \text{constant}$$, giving them identical slope $$\frac{1}{3}$$.

The constants differ: $$3n \neq 2n$$ for $$n > 0$$. Subtracting:
$$(x - 3y) - (x - 3y) = 3n - 2n$$
$$0 = n$$
This is a contradiction for positive $$n$$, confirming no solution exists.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
